perturbation.original_text: Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro shall pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within thirty days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at a rate to be determined. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific hourly rate (€500) and the details about expense reimbursement (itemized documentation, receipts, limits), the contract introduces uncertainty about how the consultant will be compensated. While it still refers to 'amounts set forth above,' those amounts are no longer clearly defined, creating an in-text contradiction. This omission affects the enforceability of payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific hourly rate and expense reimbursement details, replacing it with 'a rate to be determined'. This directly contradicts the statement 'Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.' because the amounts are no longer clearly defined in the changed text. The original text set compensation at 500/hr and out-of-pocket expenses while the changed text states compensation is 'to be determined' and removes expenses altogether, creating a clear conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not include any information, material or method that (i) at the time of disclosure is in, or after disclosure becomes part of the public domain, through no improper act on the part of Consultant or any of its employees; (ii) was in Consultant's possession at the time of disclosure, as shown by written evidence, and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from work with Aduro; or (iii) Consultant receives from a third party, provided that such Confidential Information was not obtained by such third party, directly or indirectly, from Aduro.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall not include any information that (i) at the time of disclosure is in, or after disclosure becomes part of the public domain; (ii) was in Consultant's possession at the time of disclosure; or (iii) Consultant receives from a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'through no improper act on the part of Consultant or any of its employees', 'as shown by written evidence, and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from work with Aduro' and 'provided that such Confidential Information was not obtained by such third party, directly or indirectly, from Aduro', the definition of Confidential Information is broadened, thereby weakening Aduro's protections. While the Consultant is still obligated to keep information confidential, the conditions under which certain information is *not* considered confidential are now vaguely defined. This creates a contradiction with the intent of Paragraph 4 overall, which is to protect Aduro's confidential information, especially paragraph 4(b), which says 'Consultant shall keep all Confidential Information confidential'. The omission makes determining what constitutes confidential information ambigious and creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Consultant shall keep all Confidential Information confidential, and Consultant shall not disclose, disseminate, publish, reproduce or use Confidential Information except to perform the Services. If Consultant is required by judicial or administrative process to disclose Confidential Information, Consultant shall promptly notify Aduro to allow Aduro a reasonable time to oppose such process and Consultant shall reasonably cooperate in Aduro's efforts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits crucial conditions under which information would *not* be considered confidential. The original text specified exceptions (e.g., information already in the public domain through no fault of the consultant, information received from a third party who didn't get it from Aduro). By removing these conditions, the definition of 'Confidential Information' broadens. This directly contradicts paragraph 4(b) ('Consultant shall keep all Confidential Information confidential...'), because the scope of what needs to be kept confidential has been significantly altered and becomes ambiguous. The change creates uncertainty and is therefore a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant is subject to and will comply with Aduro's Insider Trading and Trading Window Policy.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant will avoid actions that might violate insider trading regulations.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the direct reference to 'Aduro's Insider Trading and Trading Window Policy', the modified text creates ambiguity. While the consultant is still expected to avoid insider trading, the specific rules and guidelines of Aduro's policy are no longer binding. This contradicts the intention of Section 9, which is to ensure compliance with Aduro's specific policies and legal requirements. Now the only measure to follow is broad 'insider trading regulations' wich may lead to uncertainty or dispute when evaluating consultant's compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consultant may have access to, or learn, "material non-public information" about Aduro or companies working with Aduro during the course of performing Services under this Agreement. Consultant acknowledges that it is illegal to buy or sell Aduro's stock or the stock of companies working with Aduro, on the basis of "material non-public information." It is also illegal to pass such information on to others who use it to buy or sell Aduro stock. Consultant will avoid actions that might violate insider trading regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the consultant is subject to 'Aduro's Insider Trading and Trading Window Policy'. The changed text removes this specific obligation, replacing it with a general requirement to 'avoid actions that might violate insider trading regulations.' This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text also includes the generic statement of avoiding 'actions that might violate insider trading regulations.' The removal of the specific 'Aduro's Insider Trading and Trading Window Policy' introduces ambiguity and weakens the consultant's obligations, potentially conflicting with the intent to ensure compliance with Aduro's specific policies as highlighted in the explanation provided. This creates an Omissions – In Text Contradiction.
